hard.next_step: 6/22 MC – Confirmed meeting with Miroslava, Christian, Olivia, and Jorge for Tuesday at 11:00 AM MT. Objective is to conduct an initial business discovery session to better understand the initiative, key drivers, stakeholders, current challenges, and overall business objectives. Aligned with the client that this discussion will remain at a high level from a business perspective, while a deeper process and technical discussion will take place once the NDA is fully executed. NDA review remains in progress, and we are expecting the signed agreement back as early as tomorrow. Primary goal for this session is to establish business alignment, and position Zycus for the next phase of discovery.
hard.ais_why:
• No Avoma recordings exist for Fragua; the two scheduled meetings either had no conferencing details or were cancelled, so there is no transcript evidence of customer AI interest.
• SF next step describes a procurement transformation RFP covering sourcing, supplier risk, eProcurement, and spend analysis - broad scope but no AI, agentic, or Merlin cues in the narrative.
• Opp is brand-new (created 5/7) at Qualified with onsite discovery still to be scheduled, so absence of AI signal is a data-quality artifact more than a clear stance.

north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer is pulling hard after a strong 23 Jun discovery, but the NDA has slipped ~10 days past its promise, blocking the RFP and demo, and the deal owner just changed from Mario Castro to Karson Keogh mid-cycle.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 7 May 2026, ~60 days old; Formal Evaluation since 13 Jun (23 days in stage). Close 30 Sep 2026 is 86 days out. LastActivityDate 2 Jul (4 days ago) is a rep-side chase, not a fresh buyer touch. Buyer's own stated target to close evaluations by end of June has already slipped into July. NDA promised 'back tomorrow' on 22 Jun is still unexecuted on 2 Jul. Full enterprise S2P cycle typically runs 12-15 months; 86 days to a Sep close is optimistic given the demo and RFP have not started.
evidence: 23 Jun discovery session (Crystian Panduro, Miroslava Sarahi, Olivia Guzman, Jorge Ortiz) landed well: Crystian articulated the full pain (visibility, traceability, KPI automation, manual-process reduction) and agreed to a Zycus business-case workshop.

Buyer named the competitive field on 23 Jun ('estan compitiendo con Coupa, SAP y varias'), found Zycus via the Gartner quadrant, and admitted Zycus entered 'un poquito tarde a la fiesta' after evaluation had begun with other vendors.

NDA execution stalled: on 22 Jun Zycus expected the signed agreement 'tomorrow'; the 25 Jun draft requires 3 originals signed in blue ink and physically couriered; as of 2 Jul the buyer (Mariana) is still chasing Zycus for the signed document. RFP, SaaS evaluation and security RFI are all gated behind it.

Owner changed Mario Castro to Karson Keogh on 26 Jun; the 2 Jul email tells the buyer Mario 'ha tomado una nueva oportunidad profesional'. Mario held deep Spanish/LATAM rapport and prior Fragua ties from his Adobe days, so continuity is at risk until Karson re-engages.

No quantified savings target: Crystian said he lacks a target number and asked Zycus to help build the case, floating a market benchmark of 2-7 percent savings ('han incrementado su nivel de ahorro entre un 2 y un 7 por 100').

metrics.narrative: Crystian Panduro articulated rich qualitative pain on 23 Jun but no hard numbers: he wants end-to-end visibility and traceability of the procurement cycle, automated KPI calculation (fill rate, delivery times, attention/cycle times, savings per supplier), and reduction of manual work so buyers can focus on strategic sourcing ('disminuir tareas operativas... para enfocar al equipo de compras en tareas mas estrategicas'). Tenders are run today in Excel saved on the buyer's machine, which he called out as a traceability failure. Crucially he admitted he cannot quantify the target: 'Tengo expectativa, pero desconozco a que podria llegar', and asked Zycus to help justify the $200K investment using market data, floating a 2-7 percent savings range. Metrics are therefore partial (clear pain, no quantified value case), and the move is to co-build the ROI model in the offered business-case workshop.

economic_buyer.narrative: When Mario asked directly who is the executive sponsor and 'quien esta firmando el cheque', the room answered 'Compras? Crystian' and confirmed the initiative is owned by Procurement (Compras Institucionales), not IT or Legal. Crystian Jesus Panduro Morales, who introduced himself as Subgerente de Compras Institucionales, is the de-facto business owner and sponsor: he framed the vision, the pain, the success criteria and agreed to the commercial workshop. However 'subgerente' is a deputy-manager level, so the final budget-signature authority above him (a Gerente or Director de Compras) has not been named or engaged, and for a $200K enterprise deal that approval chain matters. EB is therefore identified at the sponsor level (Compras/Crystian) but the check-signer above him is the open gap, and mapping that person is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026 (Jorge Ortiz, Crystian Panduro)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are concrete and buyer-stated. Fragua runs a structured, multi-instrument process: an RFP of roughly 80 business and technical (integration) requirements, a SaaS platform evaluation of ~13 Excel tabs at ~30 questions each for IT/infrastructure/telecom, and a separate security RFI of ~20 questions sent by the security team. Crystian named the five demo focus areas Zycus must address: tenders (licitaciones), catalog procurement, supplier collaboration, document/information management, and KPIs/analytics, and confirmed none can be dropped ('esas 5, esos 5 puntos, son necesarios'). ERP integration is explicitly the highest-value criterion ('donde estan los hoyos mas grandes'), though the buyer would not name the ERP pre-NDA. Success will be measured against a business case Crystian wants Zycus to help build.
sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026 (Crystian Panduro, Miroslava Sarahi)

decision_process.narrative: The process reads clearly from the 23 Jun call: NDA execution first (blocking everything), then release of the RFP + SaaS evaluation + security RFI, a first functional/business demo for Compras Institucionales, and a later, more technical demo/POC with database, server, telco, security and business-analyst teams once use cases are shared. The buyer defines the POC as a guided, hands-on technical demo rather than a full integrated pilot, because ERP integration would take too long ('nos va a llevar mucho tiempo hacer la integracion'). Buyer wanted to close evaluations by end of June, an aggressive target Mario openly pushed back on as unrealistic for a transformation program. Approval sits with Procurement leadership.
sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026 (Crystian Panduro, Jorge Ortiz)

paper_process.narrative: The only paper mechanic visible so far is the NDA, and it is the current choke point: Fragua Legal is generating the NDA in Qdoc; the executed draft requires 3 originals signed in blue ink and physically couriered to a Mexico City address (Calle Mar Baltico). On 23 Jun the buyer offered to accelerate by sharing the RFP as soon as Zycus signs its counterpart ('con que firmen ustedes, ya podemos compartirles'), rather than waiting for both internal signatures. As of 2 Jul the signed NDA is still outstanding and the buyer is chasing it. Beyond the NDA, no MSA/SOW, procurement-onboarding or contracting mechanics have surfaced yet (correct for this early Formal Evaluation stage). Visibility into the full paper path will come with the RFP.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-articulated and owned by the relevant stakeholder (Compras Institucionales). Fragua has run its ERP for 8-9 years and recently moved it to the cloud, but the ERP does not cover the procurement lifecycle: they need a tool to complement the ERP for sourcing, supplier onboarding, tender management, proposal comparison and a document repository. Today tenders live in Excel on individual machines with no traceability ('despues de un ano, dos anos, andar buscando donde quedo la licitacion'); supplier communication is by email and phone; KPIs (savings, delivery times, cycle times) are calculated manually and are error-prone. The top-ranked priority Crystian stated is visibility and traceability of process status, then reduction of manual work, then supplier collaboration. Supplier discovery/sourcing is explicitly NOT a priority ('la busqueda de proveedores no es como la prioridad'); a supplier-evaluation/ranking capability was named as a welcome plus.

champion.narrative: Miroslava Sarahi Cabrera Navarro (Analista de Negocios TI) is the developing champion/coordinator: she initiated contact, organized and drove the calendar for the 15 Jun and 23 Jun sessions, brought the wider committee into the room, and remains the consistent email thread through late June. What has developed is reliable access and multi-stakeholder facilitation; what is still missing is seniority and demonstrated internal advocacy (she is analyst-level and does not control budget). The stronger internal advocate is Crystian Panduro, who owns the initiative and the pain narrative for Compras. The gap is that neither has yet introduced a budget-signing executive, and the person who built the deepest rapport (Mario) has left the account, so the champion relationship must now be transferred to Karson Keogh before it cools.

competition.narrative: This is an active multi-vendor RFP, not a sole-source. On 23 Jun the buyer stated plainly that Zycus is 'compitiendo con Coupa, SAP y varias' and that they found Zycus via the Gartner Magic Quadrant as a 'proveedor fuerte'. No competitor was praised or preferred, but Zycus entered late, after evaluation had already begun with other vendors ('ya empezaron la evaluacion con algunos de los proveedores'). Zycus's wedge, per Mario's positioning, is 20+ years of procurement-native software built by procurement people, 100 percent modular so the buyer only pays for what they need, deep ERP-integration capability, and the ability to custom-develop (Heineken Mexico's 200K-supplier example). The move is a deliberate differentiation play vs Coupa and SAP anchored on ERP integration depth and configurability.
sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026 (Miroslava Sarahi, Crystian Panduro)

deal_movement.summary: Deal advanced from Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 13 Jun and held a substantive discovery on 23 Jun that surfaced the pain, the evaluation process and the competitive field. Since then two frictions appeared: the owner changed from Mario Castro to Karson Keogh on 26 Jun, and the NDA that gates the RFP is still unexecuted as of 2 Jul. Buyer engagement remains strong and rep-led follow-up is live.

items.change: Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation
Business discovery session held; full pain, evaluation process and competitors (Coupa, SAP) mapped; Zycus business-case workshop agreed
Owner changed Mario Castro to Karson Keogh; account moved to New Markets division
NDA still unexecuted; buyer chasing Zycus for the signed document, RFP release still blocked

competitive_position.summary: Active multi-vendor RFP surfaced on the 23 Jun discovery call: the buyer named Coupa and SAP as the other contenders and found Zycus via the Gartner quadrant. No rival is preferred and neither was praised, but Zycus entered late after evaluation had begun, so the current threat is timing and incumbency-of-process, not a specific winning competitor. Coupa is the single strongest current alternative by name-order; SAP is the second. The wedge is ERP-integration depth and procurement-native configurability.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: estan compitiendo con Coupa, SAP y varias, hay proveedores
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor on 20+ years procurement-native build and deep ERP integration vs Coupa's platform breadth; make configurability (Heineken 200K-supplier example) the proof point in the first demo.

competitors.name: SAP
competitors.quote: estan compitiendo con Coupa, SAP y varias, hay proveedores
competitors.how_we_win: Position modular, own-the-code development and faster time-to-value against SAP's heavier implementation; lead with the traceability and KPI-automation pain SAP under-serves out of the box.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fragua's stated priorities map well to the Zycus S2P suite, with ERP integration as the make-or-break criterion and change management flagged by Mario as an area Zycus over-invests in versus rivals.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a late-entering vendor in an active RFP where the rapport-holding owner just changed.
flags: Transfer the relationship warmly: Karson Keogh must inherit Mario's rapport with Crystian and Miroslava fast, since Mario held prior Fragua ties and native Spanish and his exit risks cooling the buyer's strong pull.
Multi-thread to the budget owner: the initiative is owned by Compras and driven by Crystian (Subgerente), but confirm and engage the Gerente/Director who signs a $200K purchase before pricing.

Win the timing race: buyer targeted end-June to close evaluations and Zycus entered late vs Coupa and SAP, so compress NDA to RFP to demo while resetting expectations that a full S2P transformation needs realistic implementation runway.

critical_signals.text: Fragua is running a multi-vendor RFP and named Coupa and SAP as the other contenders on 23 June, with Zycus invited late off the Gartner quadrant as a strong option.
The 23 June discovery landed well: Crystian Panduro laid out the full procurement pain and agreed to a Zycus business-case workshop, though no demo has happened yet.
Crystian Panduro, Subgerente de Compras Institucionales, surfaced as the initiative owner and de-facto sponsor and is highly engaged, while the check-signer above him is still unmapped.
No savings target is set; Crystian asked Zycus to help justify the investment and floated a 2-7 percent savings range, so the ROI case is still unbuilt and the RFP is blocked behind an unsigned NDA.

items.name: Crystian Jesus Panduro Morales
items.title: Subgerente de Compras Institucionales
items.sentiment: Highly engaged; owns the initiative and the pain narrative, agreed to the business-case workshop, confirmed Procurement (Compras) sponsors the deal.
items.risk: Deputy-manager level; the budget-signing authority above him is unconfirmed, so a $200K approval path is not yet secured.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026; SF email activity 22 Jun 2026

items.name: Miroslava Sarahi Cabrera Navarro
items.title: Analista de Negocios TI
items.sentiment: Developing champion; initiated contact, drives the calendar, facilitates multi-stakeholder access, consistent email thread.
items.risk: Analyst-level, no budget authority; single coordination thread and no demonstrated advocacy against competitors yet.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026; SF Next Step 22 Jun 2026

items.name: Jorge Luis Ortiz Herrera
items.title: Lider de Proyectos TI
items.sentiment: Coordinates the technical evaluation, integration scope and the multi-team POC; pragmatic and engaged.
items.risk: Technical gatekeeper; can slow the deal if ERP integration concerns are not addressed.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026; SF email activity 11 May 2026

items.name: Olivia Guzman Parra
items.title: Lider de Analistas de Negocio
items.sentiment: Miroslava's direct leader; present at discovery, supportive but low-profile.
items.risk: Engagement and stance not yet distinct; influence over the decision unconfirmed.

items.name: Mariana Cristina Garcia Barajas
items.title: Becario (intern, NDA/legal admin)
items.sentiment: Owns the NDA process on the buyer side; responsive and proactively chasing the signed document.
items.risk: Junior/admin role; the NDA she manages is the current blocker to the RFP.

champion_strength.summary: Miroslava Sarahi (Analista de Negocios TI) is a developing champion who reliably drives access and calendar but lacks seniority and demonstrated advocacy; Crystian Panduro is the stronger internal owner via Compras. The relationship risk is the owner change: the deepest rapport sat with Mario Castro, who has left the account, so the champion tie must transfer to Karson Keogh quickly.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Jun 2026; SF owner-transition email 2 Jul 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin / agentic-AI story has not been put in front of Fragua yet. Their appetite is operational automation (KPI calculation, manual-work reduction) rather than AI, and they asked to disable the AI note-taker on 15 Jun, so any AI positioning must be framed as automation of their stated KPI and supplier-ranking pain, not as agentic AI.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 4.0 / Moderate matches the calls: strong demand for process and KPI automation but no explicit AI or Merlin appetite, and a data-policy caution (buyer disabled the Avoma AI note-taker on 15 Jun). Merlin can be positioned later against the supplier-evaluation/ranking and KPI-automation asks, carefully.

scope_change.from: Full S2P suite (iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, AppXtend, iCompliance)
scope_change.detail: No reduction versus the recorded scope. Buyer deprioritized supplier discovery/sourcing on 23 Jun ('la busqueda de proveedores no es como la prioridad') and centered on five focus areas, but the modular suite remains in play and the buyer confirmed all five focus areas are required.

items.detail: NDA execution stalled: blue-ink originals must be couriered to the relevant stakeholder; unsigned as of 2 Jul, ~10 days past the 22 Jun promise, blocking RFP, SaaS evaluation and security RFI.
Owner changed Mario Castro to Karson Keogh on 26 Jun; Mario held the Spanish/LATAM rapport and prior Fragua ties, so continuity is exposed until Karson re-engages the buyer.
items.source: SF field history 26 Jun 2026; SF email 2 Jul 2026

items.detail: Budget-signature authority above Crystian (Subgerente de Compras) is unmapped; no director or C-level engaged on a $200K deal.
No quantified savings target or ROI baseline; Crystian asked Zycus to build the business case, so the $200K is not yet justified.
Buyer targeted end-June to close evaluations (already slipped) and Zycus entered late vs Coupa/SAP; aggressive close date against a 12-15 month S2P norm.
ERP integration is the highest-value, highest-risk criterion; the ERP was withheld pre-NDA and Fragua recently cloud-upgraded it, so integration scope is unknown.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer is engaged and pulling to move fast, but the last real two-way exchange was the 23 Jun discovery and 2 Jul is a rep-side chase; the owner change and stalled NDA add friction. Not cooling on the buyer side, but momentum depends on Zycus unblocking the NDA and re-anchoring the relationship.
items: Buyer requested to move fast and close evaluations by end of June (pull, not push).
Buyer is proactively chasing Zycus for the signed NDA as of 2 Jul.
Owner transition and unexecuted NDA are the two frictions against otherwise strong engagement.

items.action: Karson introduces himself to Crystian and Miroslava, reaffirms continuity, and locks the functional demo date.
items.trigger: Owner changed from Mario to Karson on 26 Jun; buyer is waiting and the rapport-holder has left.
items.expected_effect: Preserves the buyer's fast pull and prevents the relationship from cooling during the handover.

items.action: Courier the signed blue-ink NDA to the relevant stakeholder to release the RFP and evaluation documents.
items.trigger: NDA promised back 'tomorrow' on 22 Jun is unsigned on 2 Jul and blocks the RFP, SaaS evaluation and security RFI.
items.expected_effect: Unblocks the entire evaluation track and lets the buyer share the ~80-item RFP.

items.action: Book the business-case workshop with Crystian and the Zycus value expert to quantify savings against Fragua's spend.
items.trigger: Crystian agreed to the workshop and asked for help justifying the investment, floating a 2-7 percent savings range.
items.expected_effect: Builds the ROI case that anchors the $200K and gives the sponsor ammunition for internal approval.

items.action: Map and reach the budget-signing authority above Crystian in Compras (Gerente/Director de Compras).
items.trigger: The check-signer above the Subgerente is unconfirmed on a $200K deal.
items.expected_effect: Secures the approval path and reduces single-threading to sponsor level.

items.action: Run a differentiation play vs Coupa and SAP anchored on ERP-integration depth and configurability.
items.trigger: Buyer named Coupa and SAP as active contenders on 23 Jun and Zycus entered late.
items.expected_effect: Positions the Zycus wedge before the RFP scoring solidifies and closes the late-entry gap.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer contacted Zycus off the Gartner quadrant as a 'proveedor fuerte', engaged deeply, agreed to the Zycus business-case workshop and wants to move fast, but is running an active RFP against Coupa and SAP with no decisive lean stated.

business_case.evidence: Rich qualitative pain (visibility, traceability, manual KPI burden) but no quantified target; Crystian admitted 'desconozco a que podria llegar' and referenced a 2-7 percent market savings benchmark.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 48 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 35 is anchored on the stage win (48), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 14.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
